title: RV8GR page and jump trace
purpose: Package SETDP, SETPG, and J T2 traces so page-register state and PC page loading are checked together without running the full CPU.

wiring.rule: SETDP, SETPG, and J use the immediate operand as exactly one active IBUS driver during T2.
Rises at T2 end only for SETPG; PG must hold during SETDP and J.
Rises during T2 only for SETDP; DP must hold during SETPG and J.
LOW during T2 only for J or taken branches; J loads PC with {PG, IRL}.

behavior.setdp: Opcode 0x40 loads DP from the immediate operand and leaves PG and PC unchanged.
behavior.setpg: Opcode 0x20 loads PG from the immediate operand at the T2 end edge and leaves DP and PC unchanged.
behavior.j: Opcode 0x01 loads PC with {current PG, IRL}; it does not load PG from the same operand.
behavior.page_order_rule: A J immediately after SETPG uses the PG value latched by the previous instruction, not a speculative same-cycle page value.
behavior.bus_policy: Immediate U34 is exactly one active IBUS driver for every vector; DBUS is not part of these traces.
behavior.edge_policy: U23, U32, and the U1_U4 counter chain update on rising edges only; opposite edges hold in these trace vectors.

timing.frequency_boundary: Functional trace proof only; physical timing is owned by timing_margins.json.
timing.deadband_boundary: IBUS must have R/C settling and positive driver deadband before physical speed signoff; this trace proves functional owner order only.
timing.student_rule: Check which register gets the operand before checking the final PC. SETDP changes data-page memory addressing; SETPG changes later jump pages; J changes PC.

guarantees: J loads PC from the already-latched PG plus IRL
SETDP, SETPG, and J keep one immediate IBUS driver
No vector promotes physical clock speed beyond functional simulation

audience: students ages 10-15, still useful for older learners
